name: Excessive Administrative Share Access Failures from the Same Host
notes: This rule triggers when repeated failures to access administrative shares are seen from the same host.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5000752) Failure Audit: A network share object was accessed
when the event matches Share Name (custom) is any of [ADMIN$ or C$]
when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source IP and different Destination IP in 10 minutes
thresholds: when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source IP and different Destination IP in 10 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Excessive Administrative Share Access Failures from the Same Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5000752) Failure Audit: A network share object was accessed
AND when the event matches Share Name (custom) is any of [ADMIN$ or C$]
AND when at least 5 events are seen with the same Source IP and different Destination IP in 10 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Excessive Failed Attempts to Access an Administrative Share from a Single source
Event description: Excessive Failed Attempts to Access an Administrative Share from a Single source
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
